venue.name: Allen Street Grill
venue.address: 100 W College Ave, State College, PA 16801
venue.notes: Corner of Allen and College Ave — prime downtown location. Recently reimagined with a farm-to-table focus. More energy than The Tavern but still good for conversation. Good pick when you want to signal effort without going full fine dining.

venue.name: The Phyrst
venue.activity_type: live music every night, Table Wars tradition
venue.notes: Live music 365 days a year. Famous for Table Wars — when the band takes a break, each table shouts their chant at the others. Loud, chaotic, and genuinely fun if you're into it. Good for a pair that wants an experience, not a conversation.

venue.name: Zeno's Pub
venue.address: 100 W College Ave, State College, PA 16801
venue.notes: 50+ year old underground pub with an enormous beer selection and rotating live music. Low pretense, high character. The kind of place where a 2-hour date turns into a 4-hour date without either person noticing. Best for pairs who'd roll their eyes at a wine bar but still want somewhere with actual atmosphere.

persona_a.compatibility_story: Jordan brings the kind of spontaneous, high-energy presence that can pull you out of your head in the best way — without making you feel like you have to perform. They're fiercely loyal and genuinely fun, which lines up with your no-pretense values more than you might expect. The gap in energy isn't a mismatch; it's a complement.
persona_a.venue_rationale: Allen Street Grill sits right at the center of downtown State College — lively enough to give the night some atmosphere, but the corner setup and farm-to-table focus keep it grounded and real, not flashy. It's the kind of spot that signals you put thought in without overdoing it.
talking_points: You're into live music and they're deep into rap — ask them what a perfect concert night looks like for them, and what artist they'd never miss live.
You both value loyalty, just in different contexts. Ask Jordan what loyalty actually looks like in practice to them — friend group, team, relationship.
You hike to decompress; Jordan goes out to recharge. Ask them: what does a perfect low-key Saturday look like when they're not 'on'?
persona_a.logistics: Meet at the main entrance on the corner of Allen and College Ave — it's easy to spot. Aim to arrive around 7:00 PM to get settled before the Friday energy picks up. Their farm-to-table menu rotates, so ask the server what's fresh — it's a good low-stakes way to start talking. If the bar area feels too loud, ask for a table toward the back.

persona_b.compatibility_story: Alex is the kind of person who actually listens — reflective, honest, zero pretense — which means conversations go somewhere real instead of staying surface level. They're into craft beer, history, and live music, so they know how to have a good time, just on a different frequency than you. That contrast tends to make for a more interesting night than going out with someone exactly like you.
persona_b.venue_rationale: Allen Street Grill is right in the middle of downtown — prime corner spot with solid energy and a menu worth talking about. It's got the social vibe you want without being so loud that you can't actually connect.
talking_points: You're all about basketball — ask Alex if they follow any teams, but more interestingly, ask what sport or physical challenge they'd actually want to try that they've never done.
Alex is into history and you're into hip-hop — there's more overlap than it sounds. Ask them if they know the history behind any of the places or events that shaped the rap artists they've heard of.
You both value loyalty. Ask Alex: what's the most loyal thing a friend has ever done for them? It's the kind of question that actually tells you who someone is.
persona_b.logistics: Meet at the corner entrance on Allen and College Ave around 7:00 PM — it's the most central spot downtown so hard to miss. The drinks list is solid, so if you want to break the ice fast, suggest grabbing something from the bar while you wait for a table. It's a sit-down spot so pacing is relaxed — lean into it rather than rushing.
